problem_statement: The document examines how AI coding tools affect frontend software development, with particular attention to the security vulnerabilities, quality defects, and mixed real-world productivity of AI-generated code that create a need for governance and human oversight.
magnitude_evidence: AI tools 'achieve 62-84% adoption rates globally' and GitHub Copilot leads adoption at '76% among frontend developers according to Stack Overflow's 2025 Developer Survey of 49,000+ professionals'; '50% of code characters now AI-assisted' at Google
insufficiency_evidence: Stanford evaluation of 1,689 Copilot programs: '~40% contained security flaws'; Georgetown CSET: 'almost 50% of AI-generated snippets containing exploitable bugs'; Veracode: '45% of AI code samples failed security tests, with 86% failure rates for Cross-Site Scripting defenses'; 'security performance remains flat across model generations, with vulnerability rates consistently 40-50%'; Yetistiren et al.: 'only 28.7% fully correct solutions, with 51.2% requiring developer intervention'
solution_mechanisms: mandatory security scanning of AI code
rigorous human code review regardless of AI
